poem.content:
The license, faded yellow, a promise of the south,
Where fancy chickens strutted, breadcrumbs in their mouth.
I expect no gratitude, no turkey on a plate,
Just the final whisper, sealing my weary fate.

A level field of slush, where force begins to yield,
And labor's bitter song is bravely now revealed.
The wind, a hungry beast, it tears and claws and bites,
While mushroom spores ascend towards the pale, dim lights.

I find no joy in this, no pleasure to be found,
Just south of happiness, on frozen, barren ground.
Yet even in the slush, a beauty starts to bloom,
A single, hopeful bud escaping from the tomb.

I force myself to feel, to breathe, to simply be,
And dream of golden bread, and chicken, wild and free,
Enjoy the simple grace of just another day,
Before the level dusk completely melts away.
